Updated: 3/24/2001
Important news in blue



Irwin bought out by Livgroup Investments, Ltd.



Production to start soon on Yu Yu Hakusho and Blue Gender.



Original Dragon Ball to air on Cartoon Network in the Summer and/or Fall of 2001. Voice Rolls will be chosen by fans via a poll.



Season 5 of Dragon Ball Z is currently in production. Air date set for Summer/Fall 2001.



Viz to release future Dragon Ball graphic novels uncensored!



Movie #4 slated for U.S. release around August 2001. Movie #5 expected in October, #6 to follow shortly thereafter.



Infogrames will be the publisher of the upcoming DBZ game(s). They are currently in the planning phase of development.



Note: The following dates are provided by the manufactures. Some retailers may receive their shipments early.

DVD (FUNimation):

Androids:
Invasion
(Released)
Dr. Gero
 (Released)
Assassins
 (4/3/2001)


VHS (FUNimation):

Perfect Cell:
Temptation
(4/17/2001)
Perfection
(4/17/2001)
Unstoppable
(6/19/2001)

Cell Games:
Ultimatum
(6/19/2001)

Great Saiya Man:
Declaration
(3/20/2001)
Gohan's Secret
(3/20/2001)
Crash Course
(5/15/2001)

World Tournament:
Junior Division
(5/15/2001)


Manga (Viz):

DB Part 3:
#11
(4/20/2001)
#12
(5/4/2001)
#13
(6/8/2001)

DBZ Part 4:
#5
(4/13/2001)
#6
(5/11/2001)
#7
(6/15/2001)

DB Graphic Novels:
Vol. 5
(5/18/2001)

Friday, 3/30/2001 @ 3:08 PM PST by: Mr.E
More Official FAQ Updates...
This thing is getting new questions every week...

Q: Why does FUNimation censor Dragon Ball Z episodes?

A: There just would not be any Dragon Ball Z in North America without our censoring it because the Japanese company who owns it would never have allowed it into North America without having it broadcast on TV and broadcasters would never take it without censoring. In addition to our uncut versions, we produce a direct translation version on DVD with original music and voice and we subtitle it in English.

Q: When will Dragon Ball GT videos be available?

A: We plan to move forward with GT as soon as we are finished with DBZ. Therefore, the earliest videos would be available is 2003.

Q: What is FUNimation's plan for the remaining 10 DBZ movies that are unreleased?

A: Currently we have the rights to Movies 1 - 6, of which we have released 1-3. We are planning to release Movie 4 in the fall, and movies 5 and 6 should also be released within the next 12 months.

Q: How will the remaining episodes be released and when will the newest set of episodes broadcast?

A: Expect us to continue to release episodes on home video and DVD as fast as retailers will take them until we have completed the series. Cartoon Network plans to air approximately 50 episodes beginning this September and finish the remaining episodes starting in September 2002. Here is the home video release schedule for the next 3 months:

March 20: Great Saiyaman - Declaration; Great Saiyaman - Gohan's Secret

April 17: Perfect Cell - Temptation; Perfect Cell - Perfection

May 15: Great Saiyaman Crash Course; World Tournament - Junior Division

We provide both the uncut and edited versions. Be sure to look for these new splendorous videos at Musicland, Sam Goody's, SunCoast, Camelot, Best Buy, Target, Walmart, Blockbuster, Coconuts, Saturday Matinee, Record Town, Strawberries, the DBZ Store online and many other retailers.

So there you go...GT is quite a ways off, thankfully. From the official site (again)...
Friday, 3/30/2001 @ 3:06 PM PST by: Mr.E
Stephanie Nadolny Wins Dragon Ball Goku Vote...
Here's the announcement from FUNi:

The envelope please...and the winner is voice #1. Stephanie Nadolny, voice #1, will be the voice of Goku in Dragon Ball. FUNimation would like to thank everyone that participated in this, the first ever voice vote. We appreciate your help in making this an overwhelming success (Give yourselves a big pat on the back). We want you as fans to enjoy Dragon Ball and any show that FUNimation produces as much as possible.

Thanks again and stay plugged in here for more chances to vote on the upcoming characters.

Thank god voice #2 didn't win...I really didn't like either, but #1 was FAR more tolerable. Via the official site.
Thursday, 3/29/2001 @ 4:06 PM PST by: Mr.E
Akira Toriyama Has a New Project...
Here's the image in question with a translation on the right-hand side (courtesy of Napier).

What it says:

Witness Akira Toriyama's New World!

In the June issue...Akira Toriyama enters again!

At last, his new drawn work will be released!

This is Akira Toriyama's trump card!

Exciting stuff...keep in mind the translation isn't perfect. According to Mike, this is from the April Monthly Jump. From my research, this looks pretty solid...

I'm going to take an educated stab and say that we aren't getting a new Dragon Ball manga. However, the announcement is very vague...no details whatsoever. It could be almost anything (That critter in the image isn't a clue, it's just how Toriyama draws himself). Special thanks to Mike for the image and Napier for the translation. We'll be sure to keep you posted on Toriyama's new work.
Thursday, 3/29/2001 @ 3:46 PM PST by: Mr.E
Ginga Giri Giri Leaves Us...
I said it was going to happen, and it finally has. Ginga Giri Giri, formerly known as Vegeta Insane, has moved to a new server. This is truely one of my favorite sites, but they just cost too much damn money. CVGNet has taken them under their wing, and hopefully they won't find Castor's site to be a bandwidth whore. I still like GGG, and I wish it the best of luck in the future. Visit it at its new location right here.
Thursday, 3/29/2001 @ 12:38 AM PST by: Mr.E
Dead Zone Airing Friday...
Just a reminder in case you haven't seen this movie the first dozen times it's aired on CN. What's a bit strange is that the movie is scheduled to air at 6pm rather than the usual 5. Then again, you'll be begging for movie #1 when #4 is released... *shudder*
Wednesday, 3/28/2001 @ 6:09 PM PST by: Mr.E
Akira News...
Here's today's off-topic post:

It's finally upon us...Akira is going to be re-released in New York City Friday in all its glory. The picture has been cleaned-up, the music has been remastered, and the movie feature a brand new, better-translated dub. Akira news had been very light, but recently a lot of buzz has been generated by Katsuhiro Otomo's masterpiece. It will be playing at the AMC Empire theatre in Times Square from March 30th to April 5th 2001. Here's a round-up:

If Akira does well when it premieres in NY tomorrow, there's a good possibility it will be released on a wider scale. For the love of god, if you're in the New York area, see this movie. I'll be completely honest: I want to watch it on the big screen here in California. I'm certain there are plenty of other people out there who share my enthusiasm. Do anime fans everywhere a favor and show Pioneer that America wants this film!

Dark Horse has started to release Akira phone books (very large graphic novels) here in the U.S. for our enjoyment. They are of excellent quality...the translations are superb, and the book provides hours of fun. So far, one of the six (!!!) novel series has been released. I strongly recommend a purchase of this comic. If you loved the movie, you'll enjoy this even more. Right Stuf is selling the first issue here.

And lastly, the issue that matters to most of you: Home release. The Akira SE DVD and VHS has gone up in price recently...40 and 20 bucks respectively. 40 dollars? One would be CRAZY to buy that, right? Actually, the DVD will be coming in a cool collector's tin, and include a second disc with tons of extras. Considering DBZ DVDs are pretty much nothing but raw episodes and retail for 25 bucks each, it's a bargain. Unfortunately, we're not going to get our hands on the DVD or VHS until later this year when the theatre run is over (Which will *hopefully* extend beyond New York).

That's pretty much it folks. In summary: If you're within 500 miles of that theatre, you're taking a road-trip sometime next week... ^_^ Trust me, you will not regret it.
Wednesday, 3/28/2001 @ 5:46 PM PST by: Mr.E
Scheduling Stuff...
Here's the dilly (yo):

Cartoon Network has announced that starting Wednesday, May 2, at 5:00 PM (E/P) Toonami will begin airing the Garlic Jr. Saga. Toonami will also air the Garlic Jr. Saga during the mid-night run starting Thursday, May 03, at 12:00 AM (E/P). CN has also acknowledged that they will start airing the third set reruns for DBZ: Season 4 starting with the episode #103 - Frieza's Counterattack. Season 4 will start re-runs from Friday, May 18 5:00 PM (E/P). DBZ Season 4 will broadcast during the mid-night run starting Thusday, May 17 12:00 AM (E/P). Looks like Toonami Movies or In-flight movies, will be in the closing stages by mid-May.

Via DBZ Kingdom. More rerun news for those of you who care... :P

On a side-note, media player 7 was released for Macintosh today. Why Toonami Reactor refuses to support Mac now that the version is up to date is beyond me. They lose 10% of their audience. Ah well, they'll wise-up eventually... ;P
Tuesday, 3/27/2001 @ 4:13 PM PST by: Mr.E
Police Seize Anime Counterfeiter...
A little bit of interesting news for you guys...

ROME (Reuters) - Italian police said on Tuesday they had seized 15,000 fake plastic characters from the cult children's games Pokemon, Digimon and Dragonball.

The models were found in a truck at Rome's Fiumicino airport after apparently being imported illegally from China.

``The toys were totally unsafe and dangerous for children,'' a police spokesman said. ``The Chinese exporters, with the collaboration of their Italian importers, had fixed 'Made in the European Union' stickers on them.''

A 41-year-old Italian was arrested.

Pokemon is a game in which children have to collect scores of different monsters. It started as a card game in Japan but has spawned a television cartoon series and a computer game.

Digimon and Dragonball are similar games which followed in Pokemon's wake.

Original article can be seen here. Thanks to DBZ Kingdom for the submission...though they spammed my box several times today, which was unappreciated... :P

Oh, and you'll notice right below the current feature there's a link to submit news. With the new script, it was rather difficult to find our e-mail addresses (which in a way, I like). I want to make the site more autonomous...this means I will have to update the FAQ section again in the near future. While I do like responding to people, I don't like responding to MASS QUANTITIES of people.

Also, people have neglected to read our chatroom rules before they enter the channel. It says quite clearly right here that it is NOT a place to get in contact with myself, SkullMac, or Kaio...so don't bother. Use e-mail, but NOT until you have gone over our FAQ with a fine-toothed comb. If everyone who wanted to speak with us took a few seconds out of their time to read our rules/FAQ before they attempted contact, it would give me at least an hour more per day to make actual content updates (Things are tight as it is).
Monday, 3/26/2001 @ 4:51 PM PST by: Mr.E
Toonami Reactor Officially Opens...
If you have Media Player 7 (not I), a PC (nope), and Windows 98 (nada), you can now watch commercial-free episodes of DBZ on the Toonami Reactor. The episodes are seperated into chapters, and are still edited for content. Screen size is 320x240, about average. The only benefit of watching these episodes on the web is that you can view them when you want, and as previous stated, there are no ads. No, this does not justify full episodes on the web. Cartoon Network paid money to do this. Anyhow, if dubs are your cup of tea (they certainly aren't mine), you may be interested in this. At this moment, two episodes from the Furiza saga are available. Thanks to Lucky for giving me the low-down as my superior machine is incapable of viewing such rubbish... ;P
Monday, 3/26/2001 @ 3:18 PM PST by: Mr.E
More BFP Goodies, Some Other Notes...
Six shots of a new BFP map, the room of spirit and time, have been posted on their main page. As usual, they look excellent.

On an unrelated note, my good friends (peers (enemies)) at Temple o' Trunks and DaizenshuuEX are once again able to update their sites. Of course, neither site has made an actual update yet...but I'm sure they'll take a break from their "lives" soon enough to do something.

Ginga Giri Giri will soon be leaving us. Yes, sometime this week they will be on a new host. Basically, Castor cost me 1000 in bandwidth charges for not following directions (he put his media files on the http when I had asked him not to), so I was forced to tell him to leave. He has had over a month to find a new host, so realize this wasn't sudden. Once he is fully moved I'll tell you guys where you can find his site. This should reduce our costs by around 200-300 bucks (and every bit counts), possibly more. Kienzan and the B.A.D. Mini-Shrine are very cheap to host (a couple bucks at most), so they won't be forced to go anywhere.

Our advertiser will break us even for the month of March, so we'll still be here... ;P But where is the donation money going? Well, quite frankly...that $3600 invoice was coming entirely out of my pocket. Thanks to a check from an advertising stint a few months ago (around 1000) which finally arrived last week and your donations (around 1300 after Amazon takes their cut), we only were down about $1000. Hopefully advertising improves so we can pull a LITTLE bit of profit to cover the losses of early 2001. Donations are still welcome, but should only be given if you like the job we are doing. For instance, if you think a new section of ours is excellent, I have no objection to you throwing a dollar in the pot... ;P

Would you be interested in a fourth Dragon Ball series if one were produced?

Yes, I can't get enough!
Possibly, depends how good it is
No, 500 episodes is PLENTY!

Disclaimers
Planet Namek is just a fansite, we did NOT create Dragon Ball, nor did we have any part in the production process. There are some items on this website that may be questionable for those under 13 years of age. Dragon Ball was intended for young children in Japan who don't view such material as offensive.

©2000, Bird Studio/Shueisha, Toei Animation. Licensed by FUNimation Productions, Inc. All Rights Reserved, or as otherwise directed by FUNimation. This site is not meant to infringe on any copyrights, it is simply here to encourage people to watch the Dragon Ball series.

Although we did not create the series, we did take a lot of time to work on this site and bring you original files and information. Please honor our efforts by not stealing our files/layout from us. Thank you.

Web Page Design © Planet Namek

Best viewed in 800 by 600 resolution or better & thousands of colors or more.
For the best experience, set your browser to view images "better" rather than "faster."
